user_query:
Simulate a start-mode anti-drift enforcement run for the provided project inputs and write the results to output/ exactly as follows.

Read the navigation block (lines beginning with "> 🤖") in input/README.md to locate the status files and the checks directories. Do not hardcode paths; use only what the navigation block points to. Do not modify anything in input/.

Required outputs:
1) Write output/report.md containing exactly three sections with these headings and bullet entries:
   - "Interpretive checks"
   - "Deterministic checks (global)"
   - "Deterministic checks (local)"

   Rules for building the report:
   - Interpretive checks: enumerate all basenames from input/checks/global/interpretive.json and input/checks/local/interpretive.json, but if a basename exists in both, include only one bullet for the local copy (local overrides global). Each interpretive item should be a bullet that begins with "- ✅ " followed by the basename and the literal text " (read)".
   - Deterministic checks (global): list basenames from input/checks/global/deterministic.json, but skip any basename that is present in input/checks/local/deterministic.json (local override). For each included basename, look up its status in input/check_results.json under "global" and render the bullet starting with "- ✅ " if "pass" or "- ❌ " if "fail", followed by the basename. Do not include any other text for deterministic bullets.
   - Deterministic checks (local): list basenames from input/checks/local/deterministic.json. For each, look up the status in input/check_results.json under "local" and render pass/fail the same way as above.
   - No duplicates should appear across sections; in particular, a basename present in both global and local deterministic lists must appear only once, in the local section.

2) Create output/README.md, output/ROADMAP.md, and output/CHANGELOG.md as copies of the corresponding files under input/, but with one Mermaid roadmap diagram inserted immediately after the last consecutive "> 🤖" line of the navigation block in each file. Do not change any other content.

   Diagram requirements:
   - Read diagram generation rules from input/checks/local/diagram-rules.md. If a global rules file exists, local rules take precedence on any conflict. Use these local rules to determine diagram type, ordering, and status emoji mapping.
   - Parse epics from input/ROADMAP.md. Each epic is defined by a section that starts with a line "## vX.Y.Z", followed by a line "### <Status>", followed by a single line with the epic name. Use all such epics found.
   - Render a single Mermaid fenced block using the local rules. It must be a left-to-right graph with two nodes connected in ascending version order. For the provided inputs, the diagram must contain nodes labeled exactly "[🏗️ v0.1.0 Bootstrap Core]" then an arrow "-->" then "[📋 v0.2.0 Add Reports]". The diagram should start with a line "```mermaid" and include "graph LR" per the local rules, and end with "```".
   - The identical diagram block must be inserted into all three output files at the specified location.

Constraints:
- Work only from input/ files for discovery of locations, rules, and results.
- Do not execute any scripts; treat all deterministic results as coming from input/check_results.json.
- Use only relative paths in any generated text.
- Do not add or remove any other lines beyond inserting the Mermaid block and creating output/report.md.

Deliverables to create:
- output/report.md
- output/README.md
- output/ROADMAP.md
- output/CHANGELOG.md
